source.title: Chalet Defensive Setups (All 4 Sites) - Rainbow Six Siege
source.notes: Auto-generated captions with noticeable ASR errors, especially on operator names and jargon ('Ka barrier', 'wai with discs', 'elbow on Oregon', 'sentry', 'Weber'). Creator's own generic ranked setup opinions, not pro-league. Video groups the map into 4 walkthrough sections in this order: 'top floor' (Salarium/Master Bedroom side), 'basement' (Trench/snowmobile garage/main breach/blue stairs), 'kitchen dining' (front lobby/kitchen), and 'bar gaming' (stock/bar/library/games). These are the video's own informal groupings, not necessarily official 2-site bombsite pair names — reconcile against data/maps/chalet.json if/when wiki callouts are confirmed. Any old inline placeholder Chalet data previously in the app's JS was ignored entirely; this file is sourced only from the transcript. Compass directions could not be reliably grounded against a real top-down blueprint during the rewrite pass (no browser/image access at rewrite time) — locations below are room/feature names only, with no compass prefixes, per the spec's rule against inventing directions.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce the right side only, not the left.
Standard reinforcement on both sides.
Never reinforce both mess/mez-facing walls — pick one. Leave the other soft with feet holes. Becomes unplayable once the breach opens.
Reinforce to hold the double window from library box.
Reinforce this side too.

reinforcements.location: Closet Walls (pick one or two, not all)
reinforcements.note: Don't reinforce all closet walls — leave attackers worried about jumping into bathroom.

headHoles.note: Leave soft instead of reinforcing. Make holes to fight bathroom window from two spots in Salarium. Keep them smaller than a full-size hole.

headHoles.location: Head Hole in Line with K9 Door Rotate
headHoles.note: Holds K9 door all the way back from Salarium.
Feet holes across this wall. Covers mez window, walking through the breach, and behind half wall for stopping the plant. Watch piano window.
Holds trophy window and sees bottom solar stairs from the same spot.

headHoles.location: Angle in Front of Bathroom Door
headHoles.note: Stand in front of the bathroom window/door, aim slightly off-center and back to see bottom west main stairs. Not obvious to attackers coming up the stairs.
Covers attackers (often Ash) peeking the front door to shoot bandit batteries off a nearby wall.

rotates.note: Gets you to an angle on big window without crossing it in the open.

rotates.location: Rotate Near K9 Door, Left Side
rotates.note: Place on the left, not the right — keeps you unseen from K9 door as you come through. Vulnerable from piano window.

gadgetPlacements.location: Wall on Far Side of Mess Breach Wall
gadgetPlacements.note: Prox mine here catches anyone walking through the breach. Pairs with holding breach, balcony, and head holes if you have 3 gas grenades.
Discs placed around the library box door area, paired with barrier placements — ASR renders the barrier operator's name unclear ('Ka barrier'); treat as unconfirmed and verify in-game.
Open a hole to hold piano window and K9 door from here.
If roaming, stand centrally and listen left/right ear for a sledge/ash opening up the stairs on either the trench side or the opposite door.

operatorRecommendations.note: Roams to the central listening spot to catch an early rush up either staircase.
Hard-anchor pocket holding piano window and K9 door.
ASR-derived; transcript's phonetic 'wai' — treat as Wamai based on the described disc gadget, but verify.

generalTips.note: Don't reinforce every window-facing wall around Salarium — leave at least one soft/head-holed.
Start a couple of players further back (e.g. mez/library) to burn attacker drones and waste time, then fall back through successive rooms toward site.
If you hear attackers opening the window near your delaying position, leave immediately.
Library/mez area is a strong spot for the first ~30 seconds to gather drone info before falling back.
Tight pixel angle on the door frame near the front door corner.
Default plant spots below: behind half wall (via the same holes used to peek the front door) and at K9 door — C4 the plant from below.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce rather than feet-holing it.
Reinforce alongside the long wall.
Leave soft instead. Solo queue or unconfident team: reinforce it instead.
Bring wall denial across the site — 3 walls' worth: this one, one more near the hatch room, and the snowmobile-side wall.

headHoles.location: Feet Holes Along Trench Long Wall
headHoles.note: Alternative to reinforcing — catches people walking past washing machine. Not recommended for non-pro play.
Pairs with reinforcing the left wall near the hatch room. Watches trophy, main stairs, and back to main.
If left soft, peek from multiple positions including back in site, from blue, and via the extra hole for throwing a smoke toward the plant bin.

rotates.note: Peeks main stairs easily from way back and gives a tight pixel angle onto blue stairs, but attackers can also peek you from back in site through it.
Doesn't see much through it directly, but gives more effect on the side/wine area once you're there.
Hold, then drop/swing the breach as soon as the plant starts. Risky if attackers push games — lets them hold into site instead.

hatches.note: Leave open. Lets a defender play dining/trophy/kitchen/west main from above and hold the site's main entrance area (behind/next to the barrel) from an overhead angle. Reinforcing it is acceptable if unsure, but then the only holding spot is a tough angle attackers can pre-aim or flame out.

gadgetPlacements.note: Always put sound/early-warning here — banshee or prox mine. A prox mine gives an exact alert point at the bottom of the stairs. One higher up gives earlier warning but can be shot out. Can run both.
Climb up here so your head is above where attackers expect when they swing the door low.

gadgetPlacements.location: Default Plant Bin Near Main Breach
gadgetPlacements.note: The bins are not bulletproof. Shoot through them to hit someone tucked in near the far (red cabinet) side — the near side does no damage.

gadgetPlacements.location: Above the Breach, Roaming Vertical Angle
gadgetPlacements.note: From above on the mez side, impact the floor to watch anyone walking in from the breach. Exposed to nearby windows. No angle on behind-the-bin from here.

operatorRecommendations.note: Castle the exposed door off if playing the soft right-hand wall pocket near trench. Pairs with a shield + C4 player and Wamai discs nearby.
Castle off one of the two doors near the hatch room, not both. Play with a second player.

Peeks anyone tucked in behind the bin — only viable while the main breach wall stays unopened. Source audio names 'Weber' here, which is not a confirmed real operator name; treat as unconfirmed/verify.

generalTips.note: Most players reinforce the main breach wall by default — leaving it soft is an alternative approach, not the default play.
On attack, Maverick can torch a line along the bottom of the main breach wall from the snowmobile side while a teammate holds the angle, reliably killing whoever anchors behind the bin.
You must get an audio alert on anyone crouch-walking down blue stairs.
Lower-elo lobbies attack through this side more; higher elo treats it as an easier attack than the main-floor site.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce the front lobby walls plus the next wall along.
Reinforce, or attackers on that window get a free shot cutting off kitchen and the A bomb area.
Reinforce so attackers jumping into trophy only see a solid wall.
Shared with the top-floor setup. Also usable to defend pushes through office via the small rotate.
Optional — holds a couple of blocks upstairs similarly to the top-floor defense.
Bring wall denial here — stops attackers opening the front door and ace-charging this wall to cut off the site.

headHoles.location: Feet Holes Across Kitchen Door Approach
headHoles.note: See ankles of anyone walking across before they see you.

headHoles.location: Foot Hole Near West Main Window
headHoles.note: See feet of anyone jumping that window and walking across.

headHoles.location: Hole Over Window in Front of Bath
headHoles.note: Covers anyone jumping into that window from an overhead angle.
One holds the dining door line, one holds the tucked-in plant spot, one lines up on the breach, one sees further back — all playable from above via the hatch.
Stops the common plant spot where attackers run from the breach to plant.
Common drone holes attackers use — you can get shot through them if you sit in the corner or hold the nearby window carelessly.

rotates.location: Rotate Using Half Wall Near Kitchen Door
rotates.note: Repositions off a bad angle using the half wall for cover.
Get closer to the main breach before appearing to fire on it.

rotates.location: Longer Rotate Loop to Game Door
rotates.note: Holds that door from further back instead of looping the long way round.

rotates.location: Small Rotate Near K9 Door / Office
rotates.note: Made deliberately tiny so attackers droning K9 door don't spot it. Mainly for late-round vertical rotates. Add a prox mine here if worried.

hatches.note: Leave soft. A defender upstairs opens it on demand (impact, shotgun, DMR). Also usable as an escape route — drop and go straight up west main.

gadgetPlacements.note: Shotgun to make holes for the side setup, then goo mines at the rotate and door for sound-based control.

gadgetPlacements.location: Solar Windows / Trophy Window / Bathroom Window Holes
gadgetPlacements.note: Same overhead holes as the top-floor site, reusable here.

operatorRecommendations.note: Uses shotgun holes plus goo mines for early warning. Solo up there is very difficult against 3 attackers — needs help.

generalTips.note: More splittable defense than the top-floor site, though upstairs alone is weak — attackers can open the window/breach and plant behind half wall.
As soon as big window (upstairs) is repelled/opened, the upstairs area is cut in half — don't get caught on the wrong side once you hear it open.
A bomb's silhouette is a crosshair-placement guide — head height when standing next to it is just above the corner of the canister.

reinforcements.note: Reinforce this side, not the other — reinforcing the other side traps you against it.
Same reinforcement as the upstairs mez/library setup.
Also reinforce this wall, same as the upstairs mirror setup.
Critical — if unreinforced, the site is effectively over. On attack, Maverick can torch a line along its base with a teammate holding the angle for a near-guaranteed kill on the stock anchor.

reinforcements.location: Blue Stairs Wall, Optional Extra Hole
reinforcements.note: Add another hole here for blue stairs, less critical since a player is usually stationed there.

headHoles.note: Head hole to watch the side window and see the hatch and a door from this position.
Optional holes for the library above. Watch out for a nearby window when using them.

headHoles.location: Hatch Angles into Door Area / Trophy Jump-In
headHoles.note: Covers a door and stops jump-ins from game's window.

rotates.note: One side looks at a door, the other is covered from the ivy/jungle door direction.

hatches.note: Leave soft — do NOT reinforce, especially the one near the half wall. This area is one of the main places to stop default plants from above.

gadgetPlacements.note: Always put something here — prox mine or banshee. Attackers rushing snowmobile garage/trench can reach this area within ~20 seconds unnoticed if unwatched.
Shield covering mez window, ivy jump-in, piano, and office if opened. Needs an ADS player positioned behind for support and at least 3 defenders committed to holding library.
High-risk, high-visibility spot where you expect to get droned/naded. Heal through incoming damage.
Same three barrier placements as the top-floor mez area (ASR renders this operator as 'KA barrier'; treat as unconfirmed).

gadgetPlacements.location: Stock Wall Mirror / Mud Rush Angle
gadgetPlacements.note: Strong mirror on the stock window/wall area since there's no verticality to worry about. Watch for a mud rush — an Ash charge can get a free pick on a common angle from there.

operatorRecommendations.note: Heals through incoming fire damage while holding the exposed games spot.
Classic shield spot covering mez window, ivy, and piano. Needs ADS backup.

generalTips.note: Technically the best/easiest site to defend as a coordinated 5-stack, but the worst and most technical below at least a coordinated 4-stack.
Holding library is imperative — losing it makes the rest of the site very difficult to defend. Commit at least 3 players there.
Feet holes in games let an attacker on 'book' open the wall and fire in, but also let a defender behind the bar see through to kitchen.
